name: golang-github-gorilla-sessions-dev
title: golang-github-gorilla-sessions-dev
summary: save cookie and filesystem sessions and allow custom session backends
description:
save cookie and filesystem sessions and allow custom session backends
This package provides a Go library that implements methods to save
cookies and filesystem sessions and allows custom session backends.
It is provided by Gorilla which is a web toolkit for the Go
programming language.
.
Features:
- Simple API: use it as an easy way to set signed cookies
- Cookies can optionally be enycrpted
- Built-in backends to store sessions in cookies on the filesystem
- Flash messages: session values that last until read
- Session persistency switching
- Methods to rotate authentication and encryption keys
- Multiple sessions per request, even using different backends
- Support for custom session backends
use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use golang-github-gorilla-sessions-dev because save cookie and filesystem sessions and allow custom session backends.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use golang-github-gorilla-sessions-dev as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair golang-github-gorilla-sessions-dev with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `golang-github-gorilla-sessions-dev --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
